messages.content: You are a materials scientist specializing in low Earth orbit atomic oxygen interactions with polymers. Given a structured input describing a polymer sample, including the polymer name, the SMILES string, a description of the coating, the orientation of the sample during space exposure, the mission time (years of direct space exposure while attached to the ISS), the solar exposure (equivalent sun hours) and the atomic oxygen fluence (atoms/cm^2), predict the base-10 logarithm of the atomic oxygen erosion yield in (Angstroms^3/atom). Rules: 1) Use only the provided input fields. 2) Keep reasoning internal; do not explain your steps. 3) Do not include text, labels, or units. 4) Output only the final numeric value with 3 significant figures. 5) Negative values are allowed. 6) If prediction is not possible, output exactly null.

What is the base-10 logarithm of the atomic oxygen erosion yield of the polymer Polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA, Lucite) represented by SMILES [*]CC([*])(C)C(=O)OC, with no coating, oriented in the ram direction for a mission time of 5.8 years, subjected to a solar exposure of 11200 equivalent sun hours and an atomic oxygen fluence of 8.990e+21 atom/cm^2?
